speaker: narrator
text: The Deep Hall received them without ceremony.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin stepped through the threshold and felt the mountain's weight lift -- not gone, just redistributed, traded for the particular stillness of a space that had been holding its breath for centuries. His lungs remembered how to fill.

speaker: narrator
text: The ache in his arm didn't disappear, but it quieted, as if even the scale understood that something here predated its urgency.

speaker: narrator
text: Then the scale of it arrived. Pillars like old oaks, an obsidian floor that held their torchlight and gave it back doubled. Nine alcoves ringed the walls, each one a different geometry of purpose. The ceiling arched into dark too high to read.

speaker: thornik
text: "Saints and stone,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik breathed, the words barely a sound.

speaker: narrator
text: Nobody told him to be quiet. The Hall already had that covered. There was a hum here -- low, below hearing, felt in the soles of the feet rather than the ears -- and it wasn't welcoming exactly.

speaker: narrator
text: It was the hum of something that had been here long before them and expected to be here long after. They were guests. The kind the house had been built to receive, but guests nonetheless.

speaker: narrator
text: They moved inward, and the dark closed behind them like water.

speaker: narrator
text: Dawn came thin and blue through the Deep Hall's lichen glow, turning the carved pillars the color of frost-bitten iron. The air tasted clean here -- cold stone, a thread of old forge-heat sleeping beneath it, and the faint chalk-dry tang of runes that still remembered their purpose.

speaker: narrator
text: They moved like people who had run out of adrenaline and were now running on decision.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik was already at the central table, grandfather's blood-marked journal pinned open under a gear clamp.

speaker: narrator
text: Brass goggles perched on his forehead, he traced bas-reliefs with a smith's reverent fingers: dwarves and dragons bent over the same anvil; nine pedestals lifted into place; an undefined chained shape ringed by wards.

speaker: thornik
text: "Forge Road,"

speaker: narrator
text: he murmured, beard-braid ticking against his lip as he measured spacings by thumb-width.

speaker: thornik
text: "Here. Vent shafts run parallel to it -- old draught chimneys for the caldera. If anything still breathes down here, it breathes along those lines."

speaker: narrator
text: He ran his palm along the central panel, tracing figures half his height carved into basalt that had been polished until it forgot it was stone.

speaker: thornik
text: "These are the old families. Deepstone, Ironhewn, Blackvein -- the three founding clans who answered when the Concord called."

speaker: narrator
text: His voice had gone somewhere Caelin hadn't heard from him before: reverent and furious at the same time.

speaker: thornik
text: "My grandfather tried to tell the elders about this for thirty years. They called him cracked. Said he'd spent too long breathing forge-fumes and reading dead men's letters."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik tapped the journal.

speaker: thornik
text: "He wrote that he'd stood in a hall like this once, before the tunnels collapsed. Nobody believed him. He died with the journal under his pillow and his reputation in the privy."

speaker: narrator
text: The dwarf's jaw worked once.

speaker: thornik
text: "Well, Granddad. You were right about the bloody hall."

speaker: narrator
text: Serana chalked a sigil on the stone just inside the threshold: nine quick strokes, intersecting arcs that looked wrong until they clicked into simple harmony. The chalk squeaked; the symbol caught the lichen-light and held it, no divine sheen, just stubborn geometry.

speaker: thornik
text: "Secular ward,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said when Caelin looked a question at her.

speaker: thornik
text: "Old templar trick. No god required -- only ratios and repetition."

speaker: narrator
text: She dusted her hands, leaving white on steel-callused palms.

speaker: thornik
text: "It won't stop a determined thing. But it notices what crosses."

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen lowered herself beside the mark and pressed two fingers to the stone. The vine-ink along her arms pulsed a steady emerald with new threads of silver braided through -- the Deep Hall's abjurations still singing inside her since she'd touched the shields yesterday.

speaker: thornik
text: "It listens back,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said quietly.

speaker: thornik
text: "Like a skin wound scabbing -- the Weave remembers the shape of whole. These lines help it hold."

speaker: narrator
text: Her smile was small, an apology to the mountain.

speaker: thornik
text: "For a time."

speaker: narrator
text: Across the table, Caelin worked with his right hand open over a dented tin cup. The scale in his forearm sat at coal-glow, ember-motes drifting slow beneath obsidian like fireflies behind tinted glass.

speaker: narrator
text: He called a tight needle of heat -- less than a candle -- and warmed the cup just enough to steam the water without boil. A second thread of focus: a bent pack-buckle on Serana's strap, heat-softened, thumbed true, cooled with a breath.

speaker: narrator
text: The filaments under his skin tugged, then settled. Pain: there, but manageable. Like wearing boots a size too small and learning to walk anyway.

speaker: thornik
text: "Keep the increments small, the cost small,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said, mostly to himself. He tried a third exercise: a flat of cold ash on an iron pan, coaxed to lift in spirals, then allowed to fall in his open palm until it sketched Draconic for breathe in soot. The lattice stung once -- needle-pain, warning accepted.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara watched, her expression the unreadable calm of someone who'd seen a hundred magics and kept score. The tips of her fingers were darker today, necrotic black like frostbite that had learned a language.

speaker: narrator
text: She pulled her sleeves down without comment and leaned on the table as if the stone and its memory pleased her.

speaker: thornik
text: "Three out of ten,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin looked up.

speaker: thornik
text: "What?"

speaker: thornik
text: "Your control. Three out of ten. Possibly three and a half -- the ash calligraphy was a nice touch, though the Draconic was misspelled."

speaker: narrator
text: She tipped her head.

speaker: thornik
text: "You're thinking about the fire. Stop thinking about the fire. Think about the air between you and the fire. The fire is the easy part. Any fool can burn things. The space between is where the craft lives."

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan stood with his back to a ribbed pillar, shadow lying neatly at his feet, hands loose, eyes far. He kept glancing to the chalk sigil at the threshold and then away, as if looking at a knife and deciding not to pick it up.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex was already moving, energy channeled into usefulness. She tested the Hall's margins, checked for pressure plates that didn't exist, and returned with three serviceable torches scavenged from niches high on the wall.

speaker: thornik
text: "If the dwarves didn't want anyone to touch a thing,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said,

speaker: thornik
text: "they carved it so beautifully you couldn't help yourself. If they wanted you to live, they hid ventilation like it was contraband."

speaker: narrator
text: She sniffed.

speaker: thornik
text: "Smoke goes somewhere. I approve."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik snorted.

speaker: thornik
text: "Thank you. On behalf of my ancestors and basic fluid dynamics."

speaker: narrator
text: They ate a cold breakfast: hard cheese that sweated fat in the chill, bread dense enough to patch a hole, dried fruit with the ghost of sweetness.

speaker: narrator
text: The Deep Hall seemed to approve of quiet -- it made their small noises clearer: the scrape of blade on stone as Serana honed an edge; the soft scuff of Elowen's staff; Caelin's measured breathing as he asked the Ember for one more small thing and paid for it with a pinprick of fire that didn't quite become pain.

speaker: vex
text: "Short scout, right and left,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said, flipping a dagger and catching it by the spine.

speaker: thornik
text: "We don't pick fights, we don't collect curses, we come back before Thornik starts making spreadsheets."

speaker: thornik
text: "Tables,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik corrected.

speaker: vex
text: "Spreadsheets are a myth from the west."

speaker: thornik
text: "Of course they are,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex deadpanned, already moving.

speaker: vex
text: "Thornik, you're with me. If something needs unscrewing, you can talk to it."

speaker: narrator
text: They took the eastern service corridor first -- a low-ceilinged gallery, the stone so smooth it had forgotten it had ever been rough. The smell there was stranger: faint metal sweetness, like the air over a well-kept blade; a hum underfoot as if the rock carried a memory of current.

speaker: thornik
text: "Residual load,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik murmured, palm flat to the wall.

speaker: vex
text: "Conduction lines. The Hall leeches a steady trickle from micro-wards embedded in the bedrock. Old dwarven trick to keep structural fields topped."

speaker: narrator
text: He tapped the stone with a knuckle; the tone came back pure.

speaker: thornik
text: "This gallery is stable."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex crouched at a junction where the corridor branched, sniffed the air like a hunting dog, and laid a palm against each wall in turn.

speaker: vex
text: "Left smells of water. Right smells of nothing, which is worse."

speaker: thornik
text: "The nothing-smell is old vacuum,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said, consulting the journal.

speaker: vex
text: "Sealed section. Grandfather marked it with a rune I've only seen once before -- the one that means don't."

speaker: narrator
text: He adjusted his goggles.

speaker: thornik
text: "Not 'danger.' Not 'caution.' Just don't."

speaker: vex
text: "Charming family tradition."

speaker: thornik
text: "We have a lot of those. Most of them involve alcohol and regret."

speaker: narrator
text: They backed out and tried the western spur, which choked almost immediately: a mining tunnel collapsed and then fused, as if heat had melted its failure into permanence. The smell was glass and dust.

speaker: vex
text: "Not our way,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex judged, crouching at the edge of a darkness that would not be argued with.

speaker: narrator
text: They returned to the threshold, to Serana's chalk mark watching the doorway like a lidded eye.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan's shadow moved first.

speaker: narrator
text: It slid a finger-length toward the sigil -- not with the light's logic, but with its own, pulled by something that didn't care about angles or sources.

speaker: narrator
text: The chalk lines brightened all at once, not gentle, not soft: a white-hot flare that hit the nose with scorched mineral and crisp ozone.

speaker: narrator
text: The shadow recoiled in a visible ripple, as if fire had run through oil -- fleeing back around Durgan's boots, seething there until he crushed it flat with effort that made his jaw jump.

speaker: narrator
text: Nobody spoke.

speaker: narrator
text: The flare-light faded. The sigil settled back to its cold geometry. In the silence after, each of them found somewhere to look that wasn't Durgan's face -- the pillar carvings, the floor, the chalk line itself still faintly warm.

speaker: narrator
text: The kind of silence that isn't absence of sound but absence of the right words.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik had been reaching for a device that no longer existed -- muscle memory, the instinct to measure -- and lowered his empty hand slowly.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara studied the chalk marks with professional interest, her expression deliberately neutral, which was its own kind of commentary.

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen pressed her fingertips together, feeling the vine-marks pulse their quiet alarm: wrongness, wrongness, still a person.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin watched Durgan's jaw and thought: He's not fighting us. He's fighting himself. And he's losing by inches.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan broke the silence. He wiped a single red thread from under his nostril with the back of his knuckle and looked at it as if surprised it existed. His jaw worked once, then settled.

speaker: thornik
text: "It will again,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: vex
text: "Keep the marks fresh."

speaker: narrator
text: Serana's chin lifted a degree.

speaker: thornik
text: "It noticed,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said -- not accusation, not pity. Accounting. She was already reaching for the chalk.

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen touched the stone near the burned-bright sigil without crossing it, palm flat. The silver threads in her tattoos shivered.

speaker: vex
text: "It hurt the wrongness without hating the person,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, a quiet relief in her voice.

speaker: thornik
text: "I can feel the difference. We should lace the perimeter."

speaker: serana
text: "On it,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana said, already pocketing the stump of chalk and breaking a fresh stick.

speaker: thornik
text: "Corners and sightlines. Nine strokes each. We keep the geometry honest."

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara drifted parallel to Caelin as he cooled the buckle he'd mended.

speaker: serana
text: "Your restraint is very tiresome,"

speaker: narrator
text: she observed, eyes on the faint steam.

speaker: thornik
text: "Men half your age would already have tried to set the ceiling on fire to prove a point."

speaker: caelin
text: "I only just learned how much it costs,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said. He flexed his fingers, testing the lattice ache.

speaker: thornik
text: "I can't afford to show off."

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara's smile acknowledged the answer and then filed it in a drawer marked useful. She tucked her sleeves lower. If anyone noticed the way she favored her hands, they had the courtesy not to say.

speaker: narrator
text: They ringed the Hall with chalk, nine-stroke sigils at each throat where a passage met the open space.

speaker: narrator
text: The symbols gathered the lichen-light to themselves until the Deep Hall looked like a star-chart: pale constellations behind pillars and the slow, steady coal-glow of Caelin's scale. The air tasted faintly of dusty limestone and hot flint.

speaker: narrator
text: By late afternoon they had a map.

speaker: thornik
text: "Three viable routes,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said, tapping lines he'd sketched in charcoal on the central table.

speaker: caelin
text: "Smithy run is flooded two at least two spans deep -- I can hear it breathing. Mining spur is fused at the choke. That leaves us the Forge Road with a vent shaft network as auxiliary escape."

speaker: serana
text: "Forge Road takes us toward the caldera?"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana asked.

speaker: caelin
text: "Aye. Past it. There's a bridge and a long spiral stair to the east face. If it's not collapsed."

speaker: vex
text: "And if it has?"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex asked.

speaker: caelin
text: "Then we improvise. Which, I should note, has been our entire strategy since Thornwick, and it's only nearly killed us four times."

speaker: vex
text: "Five,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said.

speaker: caelin
text: "I wasn't counting the scree slide."

speaker: durgan
text: "Then five,"

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan said from his pillar, and the fact that he'd been counting startled everyone except Vex, who nodded once in grim solidarity.

speaker: narrator
text: The second meal was eaten standing, because sitting felt like surrender and the Hall didn't encourage lingering. Serana set marching order with the brisk economy of someone who had commanded columns before and preferred not to make a production of it.

speaker: narrator
text: She drew seven names in chalk on the table's edge, considered them, crossed two out and redrew them before the marks had cooled.

speaker: caelin
text: "Durgan goes center,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, not looking up.

speaker: durgan
text: "Where the ward geometry is strongest. I'll take point with Thornik for navigation. Vex has rear eyes. Caelin behind me -- if we hit something, I want the scale close enough to read the trouble before we're in it."

speaker: nyxara
text: "And me?"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara asked, from a spot that was somehow equidistant from everyone.

speaker: durgan
text: "Wherever you're most useful. Which I suspect you'll decide for yourself regardless of what I say."

speaker: nyxara
text: "Correct. But I appreciate the illusion of consultation."

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen would walk between Caelin and Durgan -- close enough to ease the one if his arm screamed, close enough to feel the other if his shadow slipped. Nobody said this aloud. Nobody needed to.

speaker: serana
text: "Rules,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana continued.

speaker: nyxara
text: "We stay within voice distance. No one explores alone -- pairs minimum. If anyone sees corruption bloom in the stone, we stop and assess before moving through it."

speaker: narrator
text: She paused.

speaker: serana
text: "And if anyone hears a voice that isn't one of us, you tell me immediately. Even if you think you imagined it."

speaker: narrator
text: She said it like someone who hadn't imagined it. Nobody asked.

speaker: thornik
text: "My cousin Brikkle,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik said into the silence that followed,

speaker: serana
text: "once tried to set marching order for a family dinner. Twelve dwarves, assigned seating, name cards, and a rotation schedule for who got the gravy first."

speaker: narrator
text: He leaned on the table with the air of a man settling into a story he'd told before and would tell again.

speaker: thornik
text: "Three fistfights before the bread course. One of the fights was with himself -- he'd seated himself across from the mirror and forgot."

speaker: narrator
text: Nobody laughed, but the silence changed texture. Lighter. The kind that lets people breathe instead of brace.

speaker: thornik
text: "The eyebrows,"

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik added, warming to it because the warming was the point -- not the story but the warmth.

speaker: serana
text: "Brikkle's eyebrows were legendary. Not for their beauty -- for their independence. They moved in different directions when he was angry, which was always. Like two caterpillars having a territorial dispute on his face. The left one went up for outrage, the right one went down for disappointment. Together they formed an expression that could curdle milk at thirty paces and once made a goat apologize."

speaker: vex
text: "A goat,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said flatly.

speaker: serana
text: "The goat had eaten his hat. Brikkle's eyebrows held a nine-minute stare. The goat backed down first."

speaker: vex
text: "This is relevant how?"

speaker: serana
text: "It isn't. I just wanted you all to stop looking like you're rehearsing for your own funerals."

speaker: narrator
text: He picked up his pack.

speaker: vex
text: "We've survived shadow-thralls, a dragon's death, a possessed mountainside, and Vex's cooking. A tunnel won't kill us."

speaker: vex
text: "My cooking was fine,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said, but there was something almost warm in it.

speaker: serana
text: "The rabbit caught fire before it reached the spit."

speaker: vex
text: "It was efficient."

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen moved to the Abjuration alcove -- the third from the left, marked with interlocking shields carved so precisely they seemed to breathe.

speaker: narrator
text: She knelt and pressed both palms flat, the way a person might press their hands to a warm wall after coming in from the cold. The silver threads in her vine-ink brightened, and she closed her eyes.

speaker: serana
text: "Storing it,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said softly, when Caelin came close.

speaker: vex
text: "The Hall's memory of what 'intact' feels like. The Weave is frayed everywhere else, but here it remembers being whole. If I carry that memory, I can remind damaged stone of its own strength. Briefly."

speaker: serana
text: "Does it cost you?"

speaker: narrator
text: She opened her eyes. Two of the vine-marks on her left forearm had gone from emerald to a duller sage -- not blackened, but tired.

speaker: vex
text: "Everything costs,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said.

speaker: serana
text: "The question is whether you can afford it when it matters."

speaker: narrator
text: Across the Hall, Nyxara produced her black coin again and held it out to Caelin.

speaker: vex
text: "Lesson two."

speaker: narrator
text: She placed the coin flat on his open palm.

speaker: serana
text: "Heat the left half. Cool the right half. Simultaneously."

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin stared at her.

speaker: vex
text: "That's contradictory."

speaker: serana
text: "Congratulations. You've identified the point."

speaker: narrator
text: She tapped the coin with a darkened fingertip.

speaker: vex
text: "Fire is a child's magic -- hot things get hotter, cold things stay cold, everyone's impressed, nobody learns anything. Air-shaping is adult magic -- you hold two states at once and let the border between them do the work. That's how you make wind from nothing. That's how you deflect without destroying."

speaker: narrator
text: Her smile had teeth.

speaker: serana
text: "That's how you stop burning yourself alive every time you cast."

speaker: narrator
text: He tried. The coin's left edge warmed; the right edge cooled; and for one stuttering heartbeat, a thread of air spiraled off the surface like smoke from a snuffed candle -- a tiny cyclone that spun twice and collapsed.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Clever,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara said.

speaker: serana
text: "I barely -- "

speaker: nyxara
text: "I said clever, not good. Those are different things. Clever will keep you alive. Good will keep everyone else alive. We're working toward good."

speaker: narrator
text: She pocketed the coin.

speaker: serana
text: "Practice. When you can hold the spiral for ten seconds, you'll have something worth using in a fight."

speaker: narrator
text: Watches were assigned in pairs. Durgan nodded and didn't argue, which was how he agreed to things that cost him.

speaker: narrator
text: The Deep Hall breathed.

speaker: narrator
text: It wasn't sound so much as pulse -- a slow press and release through the pillars as if the mountain remembered tides. The chalk sigils held their cold stars at the thresholds; the lichen dimmed and brightened with a rhythm that matched no heartbeat the living could claim.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana took first watch alone.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik was meant to be with her, but he'd sat down to adjust a lens housing and was asleep inside a minute, chin on chest, snoring with the determined regularity of a man who'd learned to sleep anywhere and wasn't about to let consciousness get a word in.

speaker: narrator
text: She let him. He'd earned the rest. And she needed the quiet for what she had to do.

speaker: narrator
text: She walked the perimeter, counting pillars, counting breaths. The chalk marks held. The lichen pulsed. Everything was steady, and the steadiness was worse than noise because it left room for the question.

speaker: narrator
text: At the threshold of the Divination alcove, she stopped.

speaker: narrator
text: She'd been avoiding this for days -- testing it in fragments, whispered half-prayers, fingers against the Silver Dawn symbol at her throat in the dark where no one could see the light fail to answer.

speaker: narrator
text: But here, in a place where the Weave still remembered what whole felt like, she had no more excuses.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Guide me,"

speaker: narrator
text: she whispered.

speaker: narrator
text: The reply came in a forked whisper that wasn't wind.

speaker: narrator
text: Shield the bearer.

speaker: narrator
text: Offer the bearer.

speaker: narrator
text: Two instructions. One voice. The same voice -- she was nearly certain now -- split down a seam she couldn't find. The first time she'd heard it, at the crater's rim, she'd thought it was her own doubt echoing. The second time, exhaustion.

speaker: narrator
text: The third time, the forest road, she'd almost convinced herself it was the trees.

speaker: narrator
text: This was the fourth time, and the Deep Hall's wards stripped away every excuse. The words came through the stone's honest geometry with terrible clarity.

speaker: narrator
text: Shield him or sacrifice him. Which instruction was the god? Which was the thing pressing up from below? Or -- and this was the thought she couldn't put down, the one that sat in her chest like a swallowed coal -- were they the same voice?

speaker: narrator
text: Had her god always spoken in contradictions, and she'd simply been too faithful to notice?

speaker: narrator
text: Her chalk snapped in her fist. Thornik shifted in his sleep; she kept her face neutral, thumb dusting grit from her glove as if that had always been the plan.

speaker: narrator
text: The ward line brightened. The geometry held.

speaker: narrator
text: Her faith did not.

speaker: narrator
text: She looked at her palm -- white dust in the lines of her hand, the creases mapped like roads she'd been traveling her whole life -- and thought: I have been talking to something for fifteen years. I still don't know its name. And it might have been two things all along.

speaker: narrator
text: Serana's hand went to the sword; Thornik's -- stirring now, one eye open, the instinct to guard waking before the rest of him -- to the little hammer at his belt. Nothing came. The silence was just silence. The mountain was just mountain.

speaker: narrator
text: She didn't tell him what she'd heard. She would. But not tonight.

speaker: narrator
text: Second watch: Vex and Caelin.

speaker: narrator
text: They sat with their backs to opposite sides of a pillar, daggers and dragonfire keeping each other honest. Vex sharpened a blade with habitual precision -- the scrape-scrape metronome, steady as a heartbeat she could trust.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin balanced Nyxara's black coin an inch above his palm, a column of warm air on one side, a whisper of cold on the other. The spiral held for three seconds this time before collapsing. Progress. Possibly.

speaker: serana
text: "Your shoulder?"

speaker: narrator
text: he asked without looking.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Attached,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said.

speaker: serana
text: "Functional. I complain later."

speaker: nyxara
text: "You could let Thornik rebind it."

speaker: narrator
text: Vex made a face in the dark and then softened it because there was no one to see.

speaker: serana
text: "He fusses."

speaker: nyxara
text: "He cares."

speaker: serana
text: "That's worse."

speaker: narrator
text: They sat a while longer, the coin circling in the slow orbit of the too-careful, the scrape-scrape of steel on stone marking seconds. The lichen pulsed. The mountain breathed.

speaker: vex
text: "My mother's notes stop mid-sentence,"

speaker: narrator
text: Vex said eventually, the words landing like she'd been carrying them for hours and decided the weight wasn't worth the privacy.

speaker: serana
text: "Page seventy-three. Last journal. She was writing about fire-kept covenants -- the old alliances, the pacts between dragons and dwarves -- and mid-word, the ink just stops. Not a period. Not a dash. The pen lifted and never came back down."

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin let the coin fall. It seemed wrong to hold magic while she was holding this.

speaker: vex
text: "Four hundred times,"

speaker: narrator
text: she continued, the blade still moving because her hands needed something to do that wasn't feeling.

speaker: serana
text: "I've read that page four hundred times. I know the pressure of her hand from the ink depth. She was writing fast -- excited, I think, not afraid. She'd found something. Connected something. And then -- "

speaker: vex
text: "Gone."

speaker: serana
text: "Gone."

speaker: narrator
text: Scrape-scrape.

speaker: vex
text: "Three weeks before the dragon started burning villages. Sometimes I think she found what Vharisax was looking for. Found it first. And that's what killed her."

speaker: narrator
text: She looked at him, mismatched eyes catching the scale's coal-glow.

speaker: serana
text: "And now I'm following the same dead-end sentence with the same dead-end questions, and the only map I have is a woman who couldn't finish writing the word covenant."

speaker: caelin
text: "I think she'd be proud of you,"

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin said.

speaker: narrator
text: The scale stung -- a pinprick, gentle. Not lie, but not the whole truth. He corrected:

speaker: serana
text: "I think she'd be terrified for you. And proud. Both at once."

speaker: narrator
text: The sting faded. Truth accepted.

speaker: narrator
text: Finally Vex said, low:

speaker: caelin
text: "You do know this ends badly, yes? The relics. The Seal. Threads like these don't tie themselves into bows."

speaker: serana
text: "I know."

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin didn't let the coin fall this time.

speaker: caelin
text: "I don't know what it looks like when it ends. But I know it isn't clean."

speaker: serana
text: "That's the first sensible thing you've said since Thornwick."

speaker: narrator
text: She breathed out, not quite a laugh.

speaker: caelin
text: "Keep saying sensible things, Ember-boy. It helps."

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara and Elowen kept companionable silence for a long time, because both understood that speech could wake things that needed to sleep -- grief, doubt, the careful accounting of what each of them was losing -- and they needed rest more than they needed honesty.

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen sat cross-legged, staff across her knees, eyes closed. The vine-ink along her forearms pulsed in counterpoint to the Hall's rhythm -- emerald and silver, a quiet conversation between the druid and the mountain that needed no interpreter.

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara sat opposite, legs crossed at the ankle, sleeves drawn carefully over her fingertips with the practiced casualness of someone who'd turned concealment into habit. She watched the chalk mark nearest the Necromancy alcove the way you watch a door you expect bad news from.

speaker: narrator
text: When she finally spoke, it was to the chalk mark.

speaker: serana
text: "Your threads are brighter."

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen glanced down at the silver braided through her vine-ink.

speaker: caelin
text: "Borrowed,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said.

speaker: serana
text: "I'll pay back the interest."

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara tipped her head.

speaker: caelin
text: "It hurts when you try to ease him now."

speaker: serana
text: "Yes."

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen's smile was almost tender, as if at a seedling growing in the wrong direction.

speaker: caelin
text: "So I won't try unless I must."

speaker: nyxara
text: "You'll be tempted,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara said.

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen didn't deny it.

speaker: caelin
text: "I will."

speaker: narrator
text: A pause. The lichen pulsed. Then Nyxara pulled one sleeve back, just far enough. The fingertips were black past the second knuckle now, the skin with the texture of something learning to be dead while the rest of her disagreed.

speaker: nyxara
text: "How many can you lose?"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara asked, nodding at Elowen's tattoos.

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen was quiet. When she spoke, her voice had the steadiness of someone who'd done arithmetic she wished she hadn't.

speaker: caelin
text: "Each mark is a connection to the Weave. To the circle that trained me. To the part of me that can hear what the forest hears."

speaker: narrator
text: She touched the faded sage where emerald used to be.

speaker: nyxara
text: "When this one went quiet, I stopped hearing birdsong in my sleep. That sounds small."

speaker: nyxara
text: "It isn't,"

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara said, and meant it.

speaker: caelin
text: "I have seventeen. I've dimmed three. When the count reaches nine -- half -- I won't feel the Weave at all. I'll be a woman with interesting skin and a walking stick."

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara's mouth twitched -- not a smile, exactly, but the memory of where a smile would go.

speaker: nyxara
text: "A woman with interesting skin and a walking stick is not nothing."

speaker: elowen
text: "No,"

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen agreed.

speaker: nyxara
text: "But it's not a druid."

speaker: narrator
text: The silence settled between them. Nyxara looked at her own dark fingers -- flexed them once, methodically, the way you test a hinge.

speaker: elowen
text: "My patron isn't patient. She isn't kind. She is reliable, which is more than I can say for most contracts I've honored."

speaker: narrator
text: The clinical voice of a woman discussing weather that might kill her.

speaker: nyxara
text: "At this rate, the hand goes by winter. The arm by spring."

speaker: elowen
text: "And then?"

speaker: nyxara
text: "And then I renegotiate, or I stop being useful, or she finds another carrier. Patrons are like landlords -- they don't care about the tenant, only whether the property appreciates."

speaker: narrator
text: Nyxara tucked the sleeve back with care.

speaker: elowen
text: "The question isn't how much you lose. It's what you do with what's left."

speaker: narrator
text: Elowen looked at the darkened fingers. Looked at Nyxara's face -- the mask there, and the exhaustion behind it.

speaker: nyxara
text: "My circle-mother told me something the day the circle broke. She said: Decide who you are when the Weave doesn't answer. I've been working on the answer for two years."

speaker: elowen
text: "And?"

speaker: nyxara
text: "I'll let you know."

speaker: narrator
text: The lichen pulsed. The Hall breathed. Two women losing themselves by inches sat in the dark and let the silence hold what words couldn't carry.

speaker: narrator
text: Fourth watch: Durgan alone.

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik was meant to be with him -- Serana's assignment, careful and deliberate -- but Thornik had fallen asleep again sitting up, grand plans overridden by honest biology. Durgan didn't wake him. He was better alone for this. He needed to be alone for this.

speaker: narrator
text: He stood with the chalk sigil's white geometry between him and the sleeping forms of six people he could name, and his shadow studied the distance.

speaker: narrator
text: It began as a suggestion. The shadow stretched until it kissed the threshold line, then curled away like a cat from a hot stove. He felt the push -- the thing in him testing, calculating, measuring angles and distances with a cartographer's precision. He breathed. Held. Pressed it flat.

speaker: narrator
text: A vision slid across his awareness without knocking.

speaker: narrator
text: The central table. Caelin asleep on a bedroll, scale bare in the low light, the coal-glow steady as a sleeping heartbeat. Durgan's hand on a knife. Four steps. Kneel.

speaker: narrator
text: Angle of blade: shallow and long to avoid arteries, deep enough to free the hex-plate, twist at the notch where obsidian meets flesh --

speaker: narrator
text: He jerked back into himself with a soundless gasp, hand already halfway to his belt. He closed his fist on empty air until pain brought him back into the body that had not moved.

speaker: narrator
text: The visions were getting worse. Not louder -- clearer. More detailed. More instructive. As if whatever lived in his shadow had given up on temptation and moved to education, deciding that if he wouldn't take the scale willingly, he should at least know the correct technique for when his will broke.

speaker: narrator
text: A hot line opened under his nose. He wiped the blood on the heel of his palm and watched it dry black in the lichen light.

speaker: narrator
text: Minutes between episodes now. Hours before. Days once.

speaker: narrator
text: He stepped deliberately backward from the chalk and stared at the pillar carvings until the urge lost its edges -- the images of dwarves and dragons working together, the old alliances, the cooperation between things that should have been enemies.

speaker: narrator
text: He stared at that until the knife in his mind became just a knife, and the four steps became just distance, and the angle of blade became just geometry.

speaker: elowen
text: "I will walk away before I can't stop it,"

speaker: narrator
text: he told the stone under his breath, and the mountain, practical and ancient, declined to promise anything.

speaker: narrator
text: He stood until false dawn turned the lichen grey. He did not sleep. He did not move. He held the door closed against something that pushed harder every day, and counted the cost in blood that dried black on his palm.

speaker: narrator
text: The breathing of the Hall changed around false dawn. Not louder -- quicker. Someone turned over in a bedroll and muttered a name. The lichen dimmed, then steadied.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin woke from a dream that was only a pressure: Thud. Not near. Not far. Not a heart but something that had decided the rhythm of a heart was a good lie. Once, twice, and then nothing, as if it had leaned close to listen and then, disappointed, moved on.

speaker: narrator
text: He sat up, coin in his palm, and didn't practice again. The lattice hurt the way fresh bruises hurt. Useable. Unwise.

speaker: narrator
text: They rose before the Hall would have called it day. The fire-pit stank of cold ash and juniper resin; the chalk sigils were still bright but scuffed at two thresholds as if something had pressed there and reconsidered.

speaker: narrator
text: In the far curve of the vaulted ceiling, a hairline crack ran through one of the load ribs -- fine as a drawn thread of ink, barely there, the kind of thing that meant nothing until it meant everything.

speaker: narrator
text: No one looked up.

speaker: narrator
text: Caelin caught Elowen's eye across the Hall. She shook her head once -- not refusal, but honesty. She wouldn't ease his pain this morning. The cost was too high and the day too uncertain. He nodded back, and the conversation was finished without a word spoken.

speaker: narrator
text: They packed as quietly as seven people could. Straps pulled snug. Knots retied. The last of the water parceled and swallowed. Serana walked the perimeter and refreshed each ward with a careful ninth stroke. The chalk squeaked in the silence.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex checked her daggers with the same ritualistic precision she'd used since Thornwick -- each blade drawn, examined, returned. She caught Caelin watching.

speaker: nyxara
text: "They're not going to sharpen themselves."

speaker: elowen
text: "I know. I just -- "

speaker: nyxara
text: "If you say something sentimental, I will use one of these very sharp daggers to perform surgery on your personality. Consider that my emotional range for the morning."

speaker: elowen
text: "Understood."

speaker: narrator
text: Thornik had his charcoal map rolled and stowed, the journal sealed in its oilcloth, every remaining tool accounted for.

speaker: narrator
text: He patted his tool-harness -- five charges, two lenses, one intact device -- and nodded to himself with the expression of a man who has counted what he has and decided to stop mourning what he hasn't.

speaker: narrator
text: Durgan stood already packed at the edge of the Hall, precisely where he'd been all night, shadow flat and obedient at his boots. He looked tired in a way that had nothing to do with sleep.

speaker: narrator
text: When Serana passed him to check the last ward, she hesitated -- not at his shadow, but at his face. Whatever she saw there, she refreshed the chalk mark beside him with particular care.

speaker: narrator
text: That was when the first sound came from the tunnels: a patient scrape like metal testing stone, answered a long moment later by a tapping from the sealed mining spur, and then the slow rhythm of water displaced where none should be moving.

speaker: narrator
text: Three sounds. Coordinated. Not random, not natural -- the cadence of something that had been listening to them rest and had decided the intermission was over.

speaker: narrator
text: Vex's head came up.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Company,"

speaker: narrator
text: she said, voice flat.

speaker: elowen
text: "Positions,"

speaker: narrator
text: Serana returned, already in motion.

speaker: nyxara
text: "We move on Thornik's mark."

speaker: narrator
text: He lifted the lantern, checked the charcoal lines on the table one last time, and pointed toward the passage that would feed to the Forge Road.

speaker: elowen
text: "Here,"

speaker: narrator
text: he said.

speaker: nyxara
text: "Before the mountain decides for us."

speaker: narrator
text: They stepped into the breath of the Hall and felt it hold and release around them like a lung that knew their weight and had decided -- just this once -- to wait.
